summary: Write chunking configs for all v1 texts (one per text)
description:
## Goal

Author one `chunking/{tradition}/{text_id}.toml` per v1 text, conforming to the chunking config schema (see ticket ca06050b).

## Procedure

For each text in `sources/manifest.toml`:
1. Skim the raw output to identify the natural division (logion / tractate-section / verse / paragraph).
2. Pick the matching strategy (`regex` / `heading` / `paragraph`) and write the splitter pattern.
3. Set `section_label_format`, `max_tokens` (≤800), and `group_size`.
4. Fill `[metadata]` with tradition, text_name, translator, sections_format.

## Reference Patterns

| Text type | Strategy | Pattern example |
|-----------|----------|-----------------|
| Sayings gospel | regex | `(?:^\|\n)\(?(\d+)\)\s+` |
| Tractate / sermon-numbered | heading | `^\d+\.\s+` |
| Sutra / verse | regex on verse marker | varies |
| Prose sermons | paragraph | n/a — group_size=2-4 |

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every v1 source has a corresponding chunking config
- Each config validates against the schema
- A dry run of `chunk.py` for each text produces sensibly-bounded chunks

resolution.note: All 22 v1 texts have chunking configs in chunking/{tradition}/{text_id}.toml. Key lesson: TOML regex patterns must use single-quoted literal strings to avoid double-escaping. Corpus hermeticum: paragraph/2; gospel texts: regex or paragraph; Sefaria texts: paragraph/3.
